name: Samsung SmartThings
short_description: A smart-home platform of hubs, apps, and cloud services that connects and automates IoT devices across Zigbee, Z-Wave, and Matter.
long_description: SmartThings began as a 2012 Kickstarter-funded hub for connecting home devices and was acquired by Samsung in 2014, growing into an ambient automation layer for the home spanning lights, locks, sensors, appliances, and cameras. Rather than embodying intelligence in a single body, it distributes sensing and action across a room-scale ecology of objects coordinated through a hub, mobile app, and cloud. It matters for physical AI in daily life because it is one of the largest deployed examples of infrastructural, environment-scale intelligence: AI that perceives household state through many sensors and acts through many actuators, largely invisibly and proactively, on the user's behalf.

images.caption: The Samsung SmartThings wordmark logo.
images.alt_text: The SmartThings logo: the word 'SmartThings' in dark lowercase lettering on a white background.

sources.title: SmartThings
sources.access_notes: Founding (2012), 2013 product launch, 2014 Samsung acquisition, Zigbee/Z-Wave/Matter support.

interactions.title: The morning the house wakes you up
interactions.description: Set a routine so that at your alarm time the bedroom lights fade up, the blinds open, and the coffee maker switches on — a single chain of devices firing on a time trigger.

interactions.title: Describe the automation in plain English
interactions.description: Type "turn off all the lights when I leave the house" into the Routine Creation Assistant and SmartThings assembles the device-by-device rule for you, instead of wiring each condition by hand.

interactions.title: The home quietly throttles itself at peak hours
interactions.description: With AI Energy Mode on, when grid demand spikes the fridge eases its compressor, the AC drops to an economy setting, and plugs switch off — the house shaving its own power draw without you touching anything.
interactions.source_label: Samsung UK — SmartThings AI energy saving

interactions.title: Lights and a siren the moment the floor gets wet
interactions.description: Owners pair a leak sensor under the water heater so a drip triggers a phone alert, flips on a light, and sounds a buzzer — turning a silent overnight flood into something you notice immediately.
interactions.source_label: Samsung — Home Monitoring + Water Leak Sensor kit

interactions.title: A DIY valve that slams the water off by itself
interactions.description: A community member wired four leak sensors to a cheap Zigbee valve actuator so that any detected leak doesn't just alert — it physically shuts the main, a homebrew version of a thousand-dollar commercial system.
interactions.source_label: SmartThings Community — cheap leak detection & shut-off

interactions.title: Faking an occupied house from a thousand miles away
interactions.description: People on vacation run community apps that turn random rooms on and off at irregular times, so a watching burglar never sees the tidy daily pattern that gives away an empty home.

interactions.title: The hallway that only lights up after dark
interactions.description: Chain a motion sensor to a light with a sundown condition so a midnight trip to the kitchen gets a soft glow, while the same motion during the day does nothing — built from the motion input and rule engine the hub already runs.

interactions.title: A house that notices when no one came home
interactions.description: Combine presence and motion so that if a usually-busy home stays still past an expected arrival time, it nudges a family member to check in — a gentle eldercare watch assembled from existing sensors and time-based rules.
